subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: build web assets and prepare your app for any platform targets

options.description: target engine (e.g. browser, cordova)
target platform on chosen engine (e.g. ios, android)
flag to use the production configuration
specify the configuration to use
output source maps
rebuild when files change

subcommands.name: completion
subcommands.description: enables tab-completion for Ionic CLI commands

subcommands.name: docs
subcommands.description: open the Ionic documentation website

options.description: specifies the browser to use (safari, firefox, google chrome)

subcommands.name: info
subcommands.description: print project, system, and environment information

options.description: print system/environment info in JSON format

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: initialize existing projects with Ionic

options.description: type of project (e.g. angular, react, vue, custom)
initialize even if a project already exists
initialize a multi-app project
specify a slug for your app
mark the initialized app as the default project

states.name: name
states.description: the name of your project (e.g. myApp, "My App")

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: connect local apps to Ionic

states.name: id
states.description: the Appflow ID of the app to link (e.g. a1b2c3d4)

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: log in to Ionic

states.name: email
states.description: your email address

states.name: password
states.description: your password

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: log out of Ionic

subcommands.name: repair
subcommands.description: remove and recreate dependencies and generated files

options.description: only perform the repair steps for Cordova platforms and plugins

subcommands.name: serve
subcommands.description: start a local dev server for app dev/testing

options.description: host dev server on all network interfaces (i.e. --host=0.0.0.0)
use specific host for the dev server
use specific port for the dev server
the host used for the browser or web view
do not spin up dev server--just serve files
use specific host for Ionic Lab server
use specific port for Ionic Lab server
do not open a browser window
specifies the browser to use (safari, firefox, google chrome)
specifies a path to open to (/#/tab/dash)
test your apps on multiple platform types in the browser
use HTTPS for the dev server
flag to use the production configuration
print app console logs to the terminal
use specific port for console logs server
specify the configuration to use
output sourcemaps

subcommands.name: signup
subcommands.description: create an Ionic account

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: create a new project

options.description: list available starter templates
type of project to start (e.g. vue, angular, react, ionic-angular, ionic1)
include Cordova integration
include Capacitor integration
do not install npm/yarn dependencies
do not initialize a git repo
connect your new app to Ionic
specify an Ionic App ID to link
specify a slug for your app (used for the directory name and package name)
specify the bundle ID/application ID for your app (reverse-DNS notation)

states.name: name
states.description: the name of your new project (e.g. myApp, "My App")

states.name: template
states.description: the starter template to use (e.g. blank, tabs; use --list to see all)

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: config commands for Ionic

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: print config values

options.description: use global CLI config
output config values in JSON
operate on root of ionic.config.json

states.name: property
states.description: the property name you wish to get

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: set config values

options.description: use global CLI config
always interpret value as JSON
always overwrite existing values
operate on root of ./ionic.config.json

states.name: property
states.description: the property name you wish to set

states.name: value
states.description: the new value of the given property

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: delete config values

options.description: use global CLI config
operate on root of ./ionic.config.json

states.name: property
states.description: the property name you wish to delete

the channel to check for updates from
the update method that dictates the behavior of the plugin

options.description: the group of environment variables exposed to your build
the channel you want to auto deploy the build to. This can be repeated multiple times if multiple channels need to be specified
commit (defaults to HEAD)
skip downloading build artifacts after command succeeds

the channel to check for updates from

the maximum number of downloaded versions to store on the device
the minimum duration after which the app checks for an update in the background

the group of native config variables exposed to your build

commit (defaults to HEAD)

destination of certificate file

subcommands.description: delete an SSH public key from Ionic

states.description: the ID of the public key to delete

subcommands.name: generate
subcommands.description: generates a private and public SSH key pair

options.description: the type of key to generate: ecdsa, ed25519, rsa
number of bits in the key
annotation (comment) in public key. Your Ionic email address will be used

states.name: key-path
states.description: destination of private key file

subcommands.description: list your SSH public keys on Ionic

options.description: output SSH keys in JSON

subcommands.description: setup your Ionic SSH keys automatically

subcommands.description: set your active Ionic SSH key

states.name: key-path
